info.title: Press Kits Service
info.description: API for generating, versioning, and publicly sharing company press kits

MediaKitStatus.description: drafted = generation complete, pending review. generating = workflow in progress. validated = approved and live. denied = rejected by user. failed = generation error or timeout. archived = superseded by a newer version.

shareToken.description: Public share token. Use with GET /public/{token} to access the kit without authentication.

parentMediaKitId.description: ID of the kit this version was forked from. Forms a linked list of versions. Null for the first kit in a scope.

denialReason.description: Reason for denial (when status is 'denied') or failure message (when status is 'failed').

publicUrl.description: Full public URL for the press kit page. Ready to open in a browser.

contentExcerpt.description: First ~200 characters of the MDX content, stripped of markup. Useful for card/grid previews.
contentExcerpt.example: Acme Corp is a leading provider of innovative solutions in the SaaS space, serving over 10,000 customers worldwide...

shareToken.description: Public share token. Use with GET /public/{token} to access the kit without authentication.

mdxPageContent.description: Full MDX content of the press kit page.
mdxPageContent.example:
# Acme Corp

Acme Corp is a leading provider of innovative SaaS solutions...

parentMediaKitId.description: ID of the kit this version was forked from. Forms a linked list of versions. Null for the first kit in a scope.

denialReason.description: Reason for denial (when status is 'denied') or failure message (when status is 'failed').

publicUrl.description: Full public URL for the press kit page. Ready to open in a browser.

mdxContent.example:
# Updated Press Kit

New content goes here...

denialReason.description: Required when setting status to 'denied'. Explains why the kit was rejected.
denialReason.example: Tone is too informal for our brand guidelines

mediaKitId.description: Target a specific media kit. If omitted, the latest active kit in the scope (org + brand + campaign) is used. If no kit exists, a new one is created from scratch.

instruction.description: User instruction for the generation workflow.
instruction.example: Create a professional press kit highlighting our Q1 2026 product launches and sustainability initiatives

key.description: Group key (country code, kit ID, or date depending on groupBy)

ViewStatsGroupBy.description: Group results by dimension. Omit for flat totals.

CostStatsGroupBy.description: Group results by dimension. Omit for flat totals.

mediaKitId.description: Target kit ID. If omitted, finds the generating kit by orgId.

orgId.description: Org ID fallback when mediaKitId is not provided.

mdxContent.example:
# Generated Press Kit

Content produced by the generation workflow...

pressKitUrl.description: Full public URL for the press kit page.

get.description: Returns active media kits (validated, drafted, generating). At least one filter is required. Results are sorted by status priority (validated first) then by most recently updated.

schema.description: Filter by org UUID. At least one of org_id, campaign_id, or brand_id is required.

post.summary: Create or edit a media kit (versioning via fork)
post.description:
Triggers a generation workflow for a media kit. The behavior depends on the current state:

**No existing kit in scope** → Creates a new kit with `status: generating` and `parentMediaKitId: null`.

**Existing kit is `validated` or `drafted`** → **Forks**: creates a NEW kit (new UUID) with `status: generating`, copying the content (title, icon, MDX) from the original. The new kit's `parentMediaKitId` points to the original, preserving full version history. The original kit is NOT modified or archived at this stage.

**Existing kit is `generating`** → Updates the existing generating kit in place (adds the instruction, refreshes context headers). No new kit is created.

Scope is determined by `x-org-id` + `x-brand-id` + `x-campaign-id` headers. Pass `mediaKitId` in the body to target a specific kit instead of auto-resolving by scope.

To validate the generated result and archive the previous version, call `POST /media-kits/{id}/validate`. To discard the generating kit and restore the parent, call `POST /media-kits/{id}/cancel`.

schema.description: Brand UUID — scopes kit to a specific brand
Campaign UUID — scopes kit to a specific campaign

404.description: Media kit not found (when mediaKitId specified)

get.description: Returns full media kit including MDX content. Kits stuck in 'generating' for over 30 minutes are automatically transitioned to 'failed'.

patch.description: Directly update the MDX page content of a media kit. Typically used for manual edits after generation.
Manually update a kit's status. Use 'denied' with a denialReason to reject a kit for rework.

post.description: Approves the kit and sets it to 'validated'. Automatically archives any previously validated kit in the same scope (org + campaign). Sends a press_kit_ready email notification.
Deletes the generating/drafted kit. If the kit was forked from a parent, the parent is restored to 'drafted' status.

get.summary: Get public media kit page by share token
get.description: Returns a fully rendered HTML page for the press kit. Intended to be opened in a browser.

get.summary: Get view stats for media kits
get.description: Returns aggregated view stats for the org's media kits. Supports filters (brandId, campaignId, mediaKitId, date range) and optional groupBy (country, mediaKitId, day). Without groupBy returns flat totals; with groupBy returns grouped results.

schema.description: Filter by specific media kit UUID
Filter by feature dynasty slug (stable across versions)
Filter by workflow dynasty slug (stable across versions)

200.description: View stats (flat or grouped depending on groupBy param)

get.summary: Get cost stats for media kit generations
get.description: Returns aggregated generation/edit costs by querying runs-service for all runs associated with media kits. Supports filters (mediaKitId, brandId, campaignId) and optional groupBy (mediaKitId). Without groupBy returns flat totals; with groupBy returns grouped results.

schema.description: Filter by specific media kit UUID
Filter by feature dynasty slug (stable across versions)
Filter by workflow dynasty slug (stable across versions)

200.description: Cost stats (flat or grouped depending on groupBy param)

get.description: Returns all media kits across all orgs. Supports text search on title.

delete.description: Permanently deletes a media kit and its associated instructions (cascade).

get.summary: Get latest media kit for scope
get.description: Returns the most recently updated media kit for the given org, optionally scoped by brand and campaign. Used by other services to check current press kit state.

200.description: Media kit or null if none exists

get.description: Returns the currently generating kit with its instructions and past denial feedbacks. Used by the generation workflow to build the LLM prompt.

schema.description: Target a specific kit. If omitted, finds the generating kit for the org.

post.description: Called by the generation workflow on success. Updates the generating kit with the produced MDX content and transitions it to 'drafted' status.

get.summary: Get press kit data for email templates
get.description: Returns the validated press kit data for a given org. Used by transactional-email-service to populate email templates.

200.description: Email data (all fields null if no validated kit exists)
